```text
1	#!/usr/bin/perl
2	
3	#Based on BFF script filterSam.pl adapted to be used to filter on the two ends of the read
4	#
5	
6	#command to use
7	
8	#./filterSam.pl --first <sam/bam.first> --last <sam/bam.last> -i <sam/bam.all control> -b breakpoints.txt -G guides.txt
9	
10	#breakpoints are stored as two positions - one breakpoint per line
11	#each position is in the form of chr:<start>:<end> and the two positions are separated by a semi colon 
12	#you can comment out a line by using # 
13	#eg
14	#  chr15:74318559:74336132;chr17:38428464:38512385
15	#  chr15:74318559:;chr17:38428464:38512385
16	#  chr15::74336132;chr17::
17	#  chr15::74336132;
18	# You can skip start, end and chr values if you want - the script will find all reads that match any existing criteria
19	use warnings;
20	use List::Util qw< sum >;
21	use Getopt::Long;
22	
23	#global variables
24	$gminAlignQual  =  20;  #minimum alignment quality 60 is max for minimap
25	$gpoorAlignQual  = 20;
26	$gmaxOverlapRegion = 20; #maximum length region where the two parts of fusion can overlap
27	$gminUniqueAlignLength = 20; #minimum length for each part of the fusion to align
28	$gmaxGap = 30; #maximum unaligned gap between two parts of fusion
29	$gfilterConsensusLength=50; #For consensus count only count the breakpoints that are within this number of basepairs of the most common breakpoint (-1 means count all)
30	$gverbose=""; #verbose
31	$gkeepFirstUnmapped="";
32	$gkeepLastUnmapped="";
33	
34	
35	undef %gpos1;
36	undef %gpos2;
37	#these will store hashes to names that match
38	undef %gbreakpoints;
39	undef %greads;
40	undef %gunmapped;

42	
43	my $breakpointFile;
44	my $inputSamFirstFile;
45	my $inputSamLastFile;
46	my %breakpoints;
47	$gstandardChrs={"chrX" => 23, "chrY" => 24 };
48	
49	foreach my $i (1..22){
50	        $gstandardChrs{"chr$i"}=$i;
51	}
52	
53	
54	GetOptions ("minAlignQual|q=i" => \$gminAlignQual,
55				"poorAlignQual|q=i" => \$gpoorAlignQual,
56	            "maxOverlapRegion|o=i"   => \$gmaxOverlapRegion,
57	            "minUniqueAlignLength|l=i"   => \$gminUniqueAlignLength,
58	            "maxGap|m=i"  => \$gmaxGap,
59	            "filterConsensusLength|c=i"   => \$gfilterConsensusLength,

69

87	sub findSamBamFromExtension{
88		my($file)=@_;
89		my $extension=substr($file,-4);
90		if ($extension eq ".bam"){return 'bam'}
91		if ($extension eq ".sam"){return 'sam'}
92		return ""
93	}
94	sub readSamBamFile{
95	    my ( $file, $endtype, $checkbp ) = @_;
96	    my $samBam = findSamBamFromExtension($file);
97	    $samBam || die "$file does not end in .sam or .bam\n";
98	    if ( $samBam eq 'bam' ) {
99			$gverbose && print STDERR "opening bam file $file\n";
100	        open( $fp, "samtools view -h $file |" )
101	          || die "can't open $file \n";
102	    }
103	    else {
104			$gverbose && print STDERR "opening sam file $file\n";
105	        open( $fp, "$file" ) || die "can't open $file \n";
106	    }
107	    #read in sam/bam file
108	
109	    while ( my $line = <$fp> ) {
110	        unless ( substr( $line, 0, 1 ) eq "@" ) {
111				if ($checkbp){compareSAMLine($line);}
112				else{parseSAMLine($line);}
113		
114	        }
115	    }
116	    close($fp);
117	}

123	#To access multiply index by 3
124	sub readPositionFile{
125		my ($file)=@_;
126		my %bps;
127		@{$bps{pos1}}=();
128		@{$bps{pos2}}=();
129		open(my $fp,'<',$file) || die "can't open $file";
130		while (defined(my $line=<$fp>)){
131			unless(substr($line,0,1) eq '#'){
132				parsePositionLine($line,\%bps);
133			}
134		}
135		return %bps;
136	}
137	sub parsePositionLine{
138		my ($line)=@_;
139		chomp($line);
140		my ($brk1Str,$brk2Str)=split(/\;/,$line);
141		my(@pos1)=split(/\:/,$brk1Str);
142		my(@pos2)=split(/\:/,$brk2Str);
143		foreach my $i (0..2){
144		  if (defined($pos1[$i])){push(@{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}},$pos1[$i])}
145		  else {push(@{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}},undef)}
146		  if (defined($pos2[$i])){push(@{$gbreakpoints->{pos2}},$pos2[$i])}
147		  else {push(@{$gbreakpoints->{pos2}},undef)}
148		}
149	}
150	
151	
152	sub findSAZTag{
153		my (@tags)=@_;
154		foreach my $tag (@tags){
155		  if(substr($tag,0,5) eq "SA:Z:"){
156			return substr($tag,5);  
157		  }	
158		}
159		return "";
160	}
161	sub parseSAMLine{
162	  #for the rapid filter check the main assignment and tags for pos1 and pos2
163	  my ($line)=@_;
164	  chomp($line);
165	  my (@parts)=split(/\t/,$line);
166	  my ($qname,$flag,$chrName,$pos,$mapq,$cigar,$rnext,$pnext,$tlen,$seq,$qual,@tags)=@parts;
167	  if ($#parts < 4){
168		die "bad line:\n$line\n";  
169	  }
170	  my $poorAlignment;
171	  if ($flag == 4  || $mapq < $gpoorAlignQual){
172		$poorAlignment++;
173	  }
174	  if ($poorAlignment && $gkeepFirstUnmapped){$gunmapped{$qname}++}	
175	  foreach my $i (0..$#{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}}/3){
176		unless ( exists($gpos1{$i}) && $gpos1{$i}{$qname} && exists($gpos2{$i}) && $gpos2{$i}{$qname}){
177			my (@pos1)=@{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}}[$i*3..$i*3+2];
178			my (@pos2)=@{$gbreakpoints->{pos2}}[$i*3..$i*3+2];
179			#check for posMatch in position 1
180			my $printFlag;
181			if (posMatch(\@pos1,$chrName,$pos,$mapq)){$gpos1{$i}{$qname}++;$printFlag++}
182			if (posMatch(\@pos2,$chrName,$pos,$mapq)){$gpos2{$i}{$qname}++;$printFlag++}
183				#if ($printFlag){print STDERR "$line\n"}
184	
185		}
186	  }	
187	
188	  return ();
189	}
190	sub compareSAMLine{
191	  #use after found set of potential matches
192	  my ($line)=@_;
193	  chomp($line);
194	  
195	  my (@parts)=split(/\t/,$line);
196	  my ($qname,$flag,$chrName,$pos,$mapq,$cigar,$rnext,$pnext,$tlen,$seq,$qual,@tags)=@parts;
197	
198	  if ($#parts < 4){
199		die "bad line:\n$line\n";  
200	  }
201	  my $poorAlignment;
202	  if ($flag == 4  || $mapq < $gpoorAlignQual){
203		$poorAlignment++;
204	  }
205	  my $keepPoorAlignment;
206	  if ($poorAlignment && $gkeepLastUnmapped ){
207		 $keepPoorAlignment++; 
208	  }
209	  my $keepRead;
210	  foreach my $i (0..$#{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}}/3){
211		my (@pos1)=@{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}}[$i*3..$i*3+2];
212		my (@pos2)=@{$gbreakpoints->{pos2}}[$i*3..$i*3+2];
213			#check for first match and last match or last unmapped
214		if( exists($gpos1{$i}) && $gpos1{$i}{$qname}){
215		  if ($keepPoorAlignment || posMatch(\@pos2,$chrName,$pos,$mapq)){$gpos2{$i}{$qname}++;$keepRead++;last}
216		}
217		if( exists($gpos2{$i}) && $gpos2{$i}{$qname}){
218		  if ($keepPoorAlignment || posMatch(\@pos1,$chrName,$pos,$mapq)){$gpos1{$i}{$qname}++;$keepRead++;last}
219		}
220			#check for first unmapped and keep if there it maps from right 
221		if(!exists($gunmapped{$qname})){
222			if (posMatch(\@pos1,$chrName,$pos,$mapq) || posMatch(\@pos2,$chrName,$pos,$mapq)){
223				$keepRead++; last;
224			}
225		}	
226	  }
227		if($keepRead){
228			#get rid of size field
229			my ($readName)=split(/\_/,$qname);
230			$greads{$readName}++
231		}
232	  
233	  return ();
234	}
235	sub filterSamBamFile{
236	    my ( $file) = @_;
237	    my $samBam = findSamBamFromExtension($file);
238	    $samBam || die "$file does not end in .sam or .bam\n";
239	    if ( $samBam eq 'bam' ) {
240			$gverbose && print STDERR "opening bam file $file\n";
241	        open( $fp, "samtools view -h $file |" )
242	          || die "can't open $file \n";
243	    }
244	    else {
245			$gverbose && print STDERR "opening sam file $file\n";
246	        open( $fp, "$file" ) || die "can't open $file \n";
247	    }
248	    #read in sam/bam file
249	    while ( my $line = <$fp> ) {
250	        unless ( substr( $line, 0, 1 ) eq "@" ) {
251				filterSAMLine($line);
252	        }
253	    }
254	    close($fp);	
255	}
256	sub filterSAMLine{
257	  #use after found set of potential matches
258	  my ($line)=@_;
259	  chomp($line);
260	  my (@parts)=split(/\t/,$line);
261	  my ($qname,$flag,$chrName,$pos,$mapq,$cigar,$rnext,$pnext,$tlen,$seq,$qual,@tags)=@parts;
262	  if ($#parts < 4){
263		die "bad line:\n$line\n";  
264	  }
265	  my $poorAlignment;
266	  if ($flag == 4  || $mapq < $gpoorAlignQual){
267		$poorAlignment++;
268	  }
269	
270	  my $keepRead;
271	  if ($poorAlignment && $gkeepFirstUnmapped){
272		 $keepRead++
273	  }
274	  elsif($mapq > $gminAlignQual){
275		foreach my $i (0..$#{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}}/3){
276			my (@pos1)=@{$gbreakpoints->{pos1}}[$i*3..$i*3+2];
277			my (@pos2)=@{$gbreakpoints->{pos2}}[$i*3..$i*3+2];
278				#check for first match and last match or last unmapped
279			if(posMatch(\@pos1,$chrName,$pos)){$keepRead++;last}
280			if(posMatch(\@pos2,$chrName,$pos)){$keepRead++;last}
281		}
282	  }
283	  if($keepRead){
284	
285			#get rid of size field
286			my ($readName)=split(/\_/,$qname);
287			$greads{$readName}++
288	  }
289	  
290	  return ();
291	}
292	sub findBreakPoints{
293		my ($mainPos,$flag,$qname,$pos,$cigar,$alts)=@_;
294		my $mainReversed=isReversed($flag);
295		my $mainOrient='+';
296		if ($mainReversed){$mainOrient='-'}
297		my $returnString="";
298	    my ($lmain,$rmain,$mainRefLength)=findMatchingIntervals($cigar,$mainReversed);
299		foreach my $alt (@{$alts}){
300			my ($altChr,$altPos,$altOrient,$altCigar,$altConf,$altMisMatch)=split(/\,/,$alt);  
301			if ($altConf < $gminAlignQual){
302				next;
303			}
304			my $altReversed=0;
305			my ($breakpoint,$fusion);
306			if ($altOrient eq "-"){$altReversed=1}
307	        my ($lalt,$ralt,$altRefLength)=findMatchingIntervals($altCigar,$altReversed);
308	        my $score;
309	        my $goodOverlap=checkOverlap($lmain,$rmain,$lalt,$ralt,\$score);
310	        if ($goodOverlap){
311				my @mainRefPos=($pos,$pos+$mainRefLength-1);
312				my @altRefPos=($altPos,$altPos+$altRefLength-1);
313				if ($mainReversed){
314				   @mainRefPos[0,1]=@mainRefPos[1,0];
315				}
316				if ($altReversed){
317				   @altRefPos[0,1]=@altRefPos[1,0];
318				}
319				if ($goodOverlap < 0){
320					#alt goes before main
321					if ($mainPos == 0){
322						#the main match must be written first so reverse the fusion orientation
323					   	$breakpoint="$qname:$mainRefPos[0];$altChr:$altRefPos[1]";
324						$fusion="$qname:$mainRefPos[1]:$mainRefPos[0];$altChr:$altRefPos[1]:$altRefPos[0]";
325					}
326					else{
327					   	$breakpoint="$altChr:$altRefPos[1];$qname:$mainRefPos[0]";
328						$fusion="$altChr:$altRefPos[0]:$altRefPos[1];$qname:$mainRefPos[0]:$mainRefPos[1]";	
329					}
330				}
331				else{
332					#main goes before alt
333					if ($mainPos == 0){
334					   	$breakpoint="$qname:$mainRefPos[1];$altChr:$altRefPos[0]";
335						$fusion="$qname:$mainRefPos[0]:$mainRefPos[1];$altChr:$altRefPos[0]:$altRefPos[1]";
336					}
337					else{
338						#the alt match must be written first so reverse the fusion orientation
339					   	$breakpoint="$altChr:$altRefPos[0];$qname:$mainRefPos[1]";
340						$fusion="$altChr:$altRefPos[1]:$altRefPos[0];$qname:$mainRefPos[1]:$mainRefPos[0]";	
341					}				
342				}
343				$returnString="$returnString$fusion\t$breakpoint\t$score\n";
344			}	
345	    }
346		return $returnString;
347	}
348	sub checkOverlap{
349		my($lmain,$rmain,$lalt,$ralt,$score)=@_;
350		#minimum alignment
351		if ($ralt - $lalt < $gminUniqueAlignLength) {return 0}
352		#fusion happens before main sequence
353		if ($lalt < $lmain && $lmain - $lalt >  $gminUniqueAlignLength && $ralt < $rmain && $ralt-$lmain < $gmaxOverlapRegion && $lmain-$ralt < $gmaxGap){
354			$$score=abs($lmain-$ralt-1);
355			return -1;
356		}
357	    #fusion happens after main sequence
358		if ($ralt > $rmain && $ralt - $rmain >  $gminUniqueAlignLength && $lalt > $lmain && $rmain-$lalt < $gmaxOverlapRegion && $lalt-$rmain < $gmaxGap){
359			$$score=abs($lalt-$rmain-1);
360			return 1;
361		}
362		return 0;
363	}
364	sub posMatch{
365		my($query,$qname,$pos,$mapq)=@_;
366		if(defined($mapq) && $mapq < $gminAlignQual){ return 0}
367		if ($query->[0] && $query->[0] ne $qname){return 0;}
368		#find which position is lowRefCoord and which is highRefCoord - first number is not always lowest value
369		my $lowRefCoord=$query->[1];
370		my $highRefCoord=$query->[2];
371		if ($query->[1] && $query->[2] && $query->[1] > $query->[2]){
372			#swap values
373			$lowRefCoord=$query->[2];
374			$highRefCoord=$query->[1];
375		}
376	    if ($lowRefCoord && $lowRefCoord > $pos){return 0;}
377	    if ($highRefCoord && $highRefCoord < $pos){return 0;}
378	    return 1;
379	}
380	
381	sub findOtherAlt{
382		my ($altTag,$chr,$matches1,$matches2)=@_;
383		my $nmatches=0;
384		my $chrNum=$gstandardChrs{$chr};
385		my (@alts)=split(/\;/,$altTag);
386		foreach my $alt (@alts){
387			my ($altChr)=split(/\,/,$alt);
388	 #only check for non mitochondrial and non
389			if (exists($gstandardChrs{$altChr})){
390				
391				if ($gstandardChrs{$altChr} > $chrNum){
392					push(@{$matches1},$alt);
393					$nmatches++;
394				}
395				elsif ($gstandardChrs{$altChr} < $chrNum){
396					push(@{$matches2},$alt);
397					$nmatches++;
398				}
399			}
400		}
401	 return $nmatches;
402	}
403	
404	sub findAlt{
405		my ($altTag,$altPos)=@_;
406		my @matches;
407		my (@alts)=split(/\;/,$altTag);
408		foreach my $alt (@alts){
409			my @parts=split(/\,/,$alt);
410			if (posMatch($altPos,@parts)){
411				push(@matches,$alt);
412			}
413		}
414		return (@matches);
415	}
416	sub checkMatches{
417	  my($orient)=@_;
418	   my ($altChr,$altPos,$altOrient,$altCigar,$altConf,$altMisMatch);
419	   my ($refLength,$seqLength)=findLengths($altCigar);
420	   my ($lmismatch,$rmismatch)=findCigarMismatches($altCigar);
421	   my ($lseq,$rseq);
422	   if ($altOrient eq '+'){
423		  $lseq=$lmismatch;
424		  $rseq=$lmismatch+$seqLength-$rmismatch;
425	   }
426	   else{
427		  $lseq=$rmismatch;
428		  $rseq=$rmismatch+$seqLength-$lmismatch		      
429	   }
430	   return 1;	
431		
432	}
433	sub findMatchingIntervals{
434	  my ($cigar,$reversed)=@_;
435	  my ($refLength,$seqLength)=findLengths($cigar);
436	  my ($lmismatch,$rmismatch)=findCigarMismatches($cigar);
437	  if ($reversed){($lmismatch,$rmismatch)=($rmismatch,$lmismatch)}	
438	  return ($lmismatch+1,$seqLength-$rmismatch,$refLength);
439	}
440	sub findLengths{
441	 my ($cigar)=@_;
442	 my @outputRef=($cigar =~/(\d*)[MDN=X]/ga);
443	 my @outputSeq=($cigar =~/(\d*)[MI=XSH]/ga);
444	 return(sum(@outputRef),sum(@outputSeq));
445	}
446	sub findRightRefPosition{
447	  my ($pos,$cigar)=@_;	
448	  my @outputRef=($cigar =~/(\d*)[MDN=X]/ga);
449	  return(sum(@outputRef,$pos));
450	}
451	sub isReversed{
452	  my ($flag)=@_;
453	  if ($flag & 16) {return 1;}
454	  return 0;
455	}
456	sub findCigarMismatches{
457	   my ($cigar)=@_;
458	   my (@fields)=($cigar =~/(\d*[MIDNSHPX=])/ga);
459	   my (@rfields)=reverse(@fields);
460	   my ($lmismatch,$rmismatch)=(0,0);
461	   foreach my $field (@fields){
462		if(substr($field,-1) !~ /[HS]/){
463			last;
464		}
465		$lmismatch+=int(substr($field,0,-1));
466	   }
467	   foreach my $field (@rfields){
468		if(substr($field,-1) !~ /[HS]/){
469			last;
470		}
471		$rmismatch+=int(substr($field,0,-1));
472	   }
473	   return($lmismatch,$rmismatch);
474	 }
475	
476	
477
```

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/perl
2	my ($guidesFile) = @ARGV;
3	if (!defined $guidesFile) {
4	    die "Usage: $0 <guidesFile> \n";
5	}
6	open (my $guides, "<", $guidesFile) or die "Cannot open $guidesFile: $!\n";
7	my @guides;
8	my %guides_dict;
9	while (my $line = <$guides>) {
10	    chomp $line;
11	    my ($group, $chr, $direction) = split /\t/, $line;
12	    my $chrCoordDir=$chr."$direction";
13	    if (!exists $guides_dict{$group}) {
14	        @{$guides_dict{$group}}= ($chrCoordDir);
15	    }
16	    else{
17	        push @{$guides_dict{$group}}, $chrCoordDir;
18	    }
19	}
20	close $guides;
21	foreach my $group (keys %guides_dict) {
22	    my @guides = @{$guides_dict{$group}};
23	    print "Group: $group\n";
24	    foreach my $guide (@guides) {
25	        print "$guide\n";
26	    }
27	}
28
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:54:49Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:54:54Z)**_

Here's the plan. `filterEnds.pl` outputs a list of read names that span the fusion breakpoint. I'll add a `bin/breakpointPileup.pl` that:

1. Takes that read list, the original BAM, the reference FASTA, and the same `breakpointFile` format `filterEnds.pl` already parses.
2. For each region in the breakpoint file, runs `samtools view -b -N reads.txt bam region | samtools mpileup -aa -A -B -Q 0 -f ref region -` so only the filtered reads contribute to the pileup.
3. For each position in the pileup, emits the reference base by default; if a single non-reference base appears in **>50%** of the (parsed) reads at that position, emits that base instead.
4. Writes one FASTA record per region.

Indels and pileup metacharacters (`^X`, `$`, `+N…`, `-N…`, `*`, `,`/`.`) are decoded; `*` is excluded from the per-position total. Threshold and min-depth are configurable.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:55:14Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```pl
#!/usr/bin/perl

# breakpointPileup.pl
#
# Companion to filterEnds.pl. filterEnds.pl prints a list of read names whose
# split alignments support a fusion breakpoint; this script takes that list and
# emits a consensus sequence over each breakpoint region.
#
# For every region in the breakpoint file we run samtools mpileup over only the
# reads in the read-name list and walk the pileup column by column. At each
# column the reference base is used by default; if a single non-reference base
# is seen in more than --threshold fraction of the parsed read bases at that
# column, that base is used instead.
#
# Usage:
#   breakpointPileup.pl --reads reads.txt --bam in.bam --ref ref.fa \
#                       --breakpointFile breakpoints.txt \
#                       [--threshold 0.5] [--minDepth 1] [--verbose]
#
# The BAM must be coordinate-sorted and indexed, and the FASTA must have a
# matching .fai. The breakpoint file uses the same format as filterEnds.pl:
#   chr15:74318559:74336132;chr17:38428464:38512385
# Each side becomes one FASTA record. Lines starting with '#' are ignored.

use warnings;
use strict;
use Getopt::Long;

my $readsFile;
my $bamFile;
my $refFile;
my $breakpointFile;
my $threshold = 0.5;
my $minDepth  = 1;
my $verbose   = 0;

GetOptions(
    "reads=s"            => \$readsFile,
    "bam=s"              => \$bamFile,
    "ref=s"              => \$refFile,
    "breakpointFile|b=s" => \$breakpointFile,
    "threshold|t=f"      => \$threshold,
    "minDepth|d=i"       => \$minDepth,
    "verbose|v"          => \$verbose,
) or die "Error in command line arguments\n";

defined $readsFile      or die "--reads is required\n";
defined $bamFile        or die "--bam is required\n";
defined $refFile        or die "--ref is required\n";
defined $breakpointFile or die "--breakpointFile is required\n";

my @regions = readRegions($breakpointFile);
@regions or die "no usable regions in $breakpointFile\n";

foreach my $region (@regions) {
    my $cmd = sprintf(
        "samtools view -b -N %s %s %s | "
      . "samtools mpileup -aa -A -B -Q 0 -f %s -r %s -",
        shellQuote($readsFile), shellQuote($bamFile), shellQuote($region),
        shellQuote($refFile),   shellQuote($region),
    );
    $verbose and print STDERR "# $cmd\n";

    open(my $fp, "-|", $cmd) or die "can't run pileup pipeline: $!";
    my $seq = "";
    while (my $line = <$fp>) {
        chomp $line;
        my ($chr, $pos, $ref, $depth, $bases) = split(/\t/, $line);
        $seq .= consensusBase($ref, $depth // 0, $bases // "");
    }
    close($fp);

    print ">$region length=", length($seq), "\n";
    for (my $i = 0; $i < length($seq); $i += 80) {
        print substr($seq, $i, 80), "\n";
    }
}

sub readRegions {
    my ($file) = @_;
    open(my $fp, '<', $file) or die "can't open $file: $!\n";
    my @out;
    while (my $line = <$fp>) {
        next if substr($line, 0, 1) eq '#';
        chomp $line;
        next if $line =~ /^\s*$/;
        foreach my $half (split /;/, $line) {
            my ($chr, $start, $end) = split /:/, $half;
            next unless defined $chr && length($chr);
            unless (defined $start && length($start)
                 && defined $end   && length($end)) {
                warn "skipping '$half': pileup needs chr:start:end\n";
                next;
            }
            ($start, $end) = ($end, $start) if $start > $end;
            push @out, "$chr:$start-$end";
        }
    }
    close($fp);
    return @out;
}

# Walks an mpileup bases string, returning the consensus base for the column.
# mpileup encoding: '.'/',' = match to ref; ACGTN (any case) = mismatch;
# '^X' starts a read (X is mapq, skip it); '$' ends a read; '+N<seq>' /
# '-N<seq>' are insertions/deletions to the reference following this column;
# '*'/'#' is a deletion that covers this column.
sub consensusBase {
    my ($ref, $depth, $bases) = @_;
    $ref = uc($ref // 'N');
    return $ref if !$depth || $depth < $minDepth;
    return $ref if !length($bases) || $bases eq '*';

    my %count;
    my @c = split(//, $bases);
    my $i = 0;
    while ($i <= $#c) {
        my $ch = $c[$i];
        if ($ch eq '^') { $i += 2; next; }
        if ($ch eq '$') { $i += 1; next; }
        if ($ch eq '+' || $ch eq '-') {
            $i++;
            my $n = "";
            while ($i <= $#c && $c[$i] =~ /\d/) { $n .= $c[$i]; $i++; }
            $i += int($n);
            next;
        }
        if ($ch eq '*' || $ch eq '#') { $i++; next; }
        if ($ch eq '.' || $ch eq ',') { $count{$ref}++; $i++; next; }
        $count{uc($ch)}++;
        $i++;
    }

    my $total = 0;
    foreach my $b (qw(A C G T N)) { $total += $count{$b} if $count{$b}; }
    return $ref if $total == 0;

    foreach my $b (qw(A C G T)) {
        next if $b eq $ref;
        return $b if defined $count{$b} && $count{$b} / $total > $threshold;
    }
    return $ref;
}

sub shellQuote {
    my ($s) = @_;
    $s =~ s/'/'\\''/g;
    return "'$s'";
}

```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:55:31Z)**_

Created [bin/breakpointPileup.pl](bin/breakpointPileup.pl). It chains `samtools view -N <reads>` into `samtools mpileup -aa -f <ref> -r <region>` so only the reads that survived `filterEnds.pl` contribute to the pileup, then walks each column and emits ref-by-default, alt-if-strict-majority. One FASTA record per region in the breakpoint file.

Run it like:

```
bin/filterEnds.pl --first first.bam --last last.bam -b breakpoints.txt > reads.txt
bin/breakpointPileup.pl --reads reads.txt --bam all.bam --ref ref.fa \
                       -b breakpoints.txt > fusion.fa
```

A few things worth flagging:

- `samtools view -N` needs samtools 1.12+ (you have 1.13, good) and a coordinate-sorted, indexed BAM.
- `mpileup -aa -A -B -Q 0` was chosen to keep every position and every read base — let me know if you'd rather honor base-quality / BAQ filters.
- The threshold is **strict** majority (`> 0.5`). At depth 2 a single alt won't tip; at depth 3 you need 2 of 3. Easy to relax via `--threshold`.
- Indels are decoded but not emitted into the consensus; the output is the same length as the reference window. If you want indels reflected, that's a separate pass.
- For ranges where chr/start/end aren't all set in the breakpoint file the region is skipped with a warning — pileup needs concrete coordinates.

Want me to add a step that also reverse-complements based on guide direction (from `guidesToBreakpoints.pl`) so the two halves concatenate into the actual mRNA-strand fusion?

---

_**User (2026-04-28 07:55:50Z)**_

Yes that would be great

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:57:27Z)**_

Got it. I'll extend [bin/breakpointPileup.pl](bin/breakpointPileup.pl) to optionally take a `--guideFile` in the `guidesToBreakpoints.pl` input format (`group\tchr:pos\t+/-`). When supplied, each group's parts are matched to breakpoint regions by chromosome+position, reverse-complemented when direction is `-`, and concatenated **in the order they appear in the guides file** (first listed = 5' partner). Without `--guideFile`, behavior is unchanged.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:58:08Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```pl
#!/usr/bin/perl

# breakpointPileup.pl
#
# Companion to filterEnds.pl. filterEnds.pl prints a list of read names whose
# split alignments support a fusion breakpoint; this script takes that list and
# emits a consensus sequence over each breakpoint region.
#
# For every region in the breakpoint file we run samtools mpileup over only the
# reads in the read-name list and walk the pileup column by column. At each
# column the reference base is used by default; if a single non-reference base
# is seen in more than --threshold fraction of the parsed read bases at that
# column, that base is used instead.
#
# With --guideFile (same input format as guidesToBreakpoints.pl, namely
# "group<TAB>chr:pos<TAB>+/-"), each fusion group's two halves are matched to
# breakpoint regions by chromosome+position, reverse-complemented when the
# guide direction is '-', and concatenated in the order the entries appear in
# the guides file (first entry of the group = 5' partner). One FASTA record is
# emitted per group instead of per region.
#
# Usage:
#   breakpointPileup.pl --reads reads.txt --bam in.bam --ref ref.fa \
#                       --breakpointFile breakpoints.txt \
#                       [--guideFile guides.txt] \
#                       [--threshold 0.5] [--minDepth 1] [--verbose]
#
# The BAM must be coordinate-sorted and indexed, and the FASTA must have a
# matching .fai. The breakpoint file uses the same format as filterEnds.pl:
#   chr15:74318559:74336132;chr17:38428464:38512385
# Each side becomes one FASTA record (or one half of a fused record under
# --guideFile). Lines starting with '#' are ignored.

use warnings;
use strict;
use Getopt::Long;

my $readsFile;
my $bamFile;
my $refFile;
my $breakpointFile;
my $guideFile;
my $threshold = 0.5;
my $minDepth  = 1;
my $verbose   = 0;

GetOptions(
    "reads=s"            => \$readsFile,
    "bam=s"              => \$bamFile,
    "ref=s"              => \$refFile,
    "breakpointFile|b=s" => \$breakpointFile,
    "guideFile|G=s"      => \$guideFile,
    "threshold|t=f"      => \$threshold,
    "minDepth|d=i"       => \$minDepth,
    "verbose|v"          => \$verbose,
) or die "Error in command line arguments\n";

defined $readsFile      or die "--reads is required\n";
defined $bamFile        or die "--bam is required\n";
defined $refFile        or die "--ref is required\n";
defined $breakpointFile or die "--breakpointFile is required\n";

my @regions = readRegions($breakpointFile);
@regions or die "no usable regions in $breakpointFile\n";

my %byRegion;
foreach my $region (@regions) {
    my ($chr, $start, $end) = parseRegion($region);
    my $seq = consensusForRegion($region);
    $byRegion{$region} = { chr => $chr, start => $start, end => $end, seq => $seq };
}

if (defined $guideFile) {
    emitFusedGroups($guideFile, \%byRegion);
}
else {
    foreach my $region (@regions) {
        emitFasta($region, $byRegion{$region}{seq});
    }
}

sub consensusForRegion {
    my ($region) = @_;
    my $cmd = sprintf(
        "samtools view -b -N %s %s %s | "
      . "samtools mpileup -aa -A -B -Q 0 -f %s -r %s -",
        shellQuote($readsFile), shellQuote($bamFile), shellQuote($region),
        shellQuote($refFile),   shellQuote($region),
    );
    $verbose and print STDERR "# $cmd\n";

    open(my $fp, "-|", $cmd) or die "can't run pileup pipeline: $!";
    my $seq = "";
    while (my $line = <$fp>) {
        chomp $line;
        my ($chr, $pos, $ref, $depth, $bases) = split(/\t/, $line);
        $seq .= consensusBase($ref, $depth // 0, $bases // "");
    }
    close($fp);
    return $seq;
}

sub readRegions {
    my ($file) = @_;
    open(my $fp, '<', $file) or die "can't open $file: $!\n";
    my @out;
    while (my $line = <$fp>) {
        next if substr($line, 0, 1) eq '#';
        chomp $line;
        next if $line =~ /^\s*$/;
        foreach my $half (split /;/, $line) {
            my ($chr, $start, $end) = split /:/, $half;
            next unless defined $chr && length($chr);
            unless (defined $start && length($start)
                 && defined $end   && length($end)) {
                warn "skipping '$half': pileup needs chr:start:end\n";
                next;
            }
            ($start, $end) = ($end, $start) if $start > $end;
            push @out, "$chr:$start-$end";
        }
    }
    close($fp);
    return @out;
}

sub parseRegion {
    my ($r) = @_;
    $r =~ /^([^:]+):(\d+)-(\d+)$/ or die "bad region '$r'\n";
    return ($1, $2, $3);
}

sub emitFusedGroups {
    my ($file, $byRegion) = @_;
    open(my $fp, '<', $file) or die "can't open $file: $!\n";
    my %groups;
    my @order;
    while (my $line = <$fp>) {
        next if substr($line, 0, 1) eq '#';
        chomp $line;
        next if $line =~ /^\s*$/;
        my ($group, $loc, $dir) = split /\t/, $line;
        unless (defined $group && defined $loc && defined $dir) {
            warn "skipping malformed guide line: $line\n";
            next;
        }
        $dir =~ s/\s+//g;
        unless ($dir eq '+' || $dir eq '-') {
            warn "skipping guide '$group' $loc: direction must be + or -\n";
            next;
        }
        my ($chr, $pos) = split /:/, $loc;
        unless (defined $chr && defined $pos && $pos =~ /^\d+$/) {
            warn "skipping guide '$group' $loc: expected chr:pos\n";
            next;
        }
        push @order, $group unless exists $groups{$group};
        push @{ $groups{$group} }, { chr => $chr, pos => $pos, dir => $dir };
    }
    close($fp);

    foreach my $group (@order) {
        my @parts;
        my @anno;
        foreach my $g (@{ $groups{$group} }) {
            my $info = matchRegion($g->{chr}, $g->{pos}, $byRegion);
            unless ($info) {
                warn "no breakpoint region matches guide '$group' $g->{chr}:$g->{pos}\n";
                next;
            }
            my $part = $info->{seq};
            $part = revcomp($part) if $g->{dir} eq '-';
            push @parts, $part;
            push @anno, "$g->{chr}:$info->{start}-$info->{end}$g->{dir}";
        }
        next unless @parts;
        my $fusion = join('', @parts);
        emitFasta("$group " . join(';', @anno), $fusion);
    }
}

sub matchRegion {
    my ($chr, $pos, $byRegion) = @_;
    my $chrFallback;
    foreach my $r (keys %$byRegion) {
        my $info = $byRegion->{$r};
        next unless $info->{chr} eq $chr;
        $chrFallback ||= $info;
        return $info if $pos >= $info->{start} && $pos <= $info->{end};
    }
    return $chrFallback;
}

sub emitFasta {
    my ($header, $seq) = @_;
    print ">$header length=", length($seq), "\n";
    for (my $i = 0; $i < length($seq); $i += 80) {
        print substr($seq, $i, 80), "\n";
    }
}

sub revcomp {
    my ($s) = @_;
    $s =~ tr/ACGTNacgtn/TGCANtgcan/;
    return scalar reverse $s;
}

# Walks an mpileup bases string, returning the consensus base for the column.
# mpileup encoding: '.'/',' = match to ref; ACGTN (any case) = mismatch;
# '^X' starts a read (X is mapq, skip it); '$' ends a read; '+N<seq>' /
# '-N<seq>' are insertions/deletions to the reference following this column;
# '*'/'#' is a deletion that covers this column.
sub consensusBase {
    my ($ref, $depth, $bases) = @_;
    $ref = uc($ref // 'N');
    return $ref if !$depth || $depth < $minDepth;
    return $ref if !length($bases) || $bases eq '*';

    my %count;
    my @c = split(//, $bases);
    my $i = 0;
    while ($i <= $#c) {
        my $ch = $c[$i];
        if ($ch eq '^') { $i += 2; next; }
        if ($ch eq '$') { $i += 1; next; }
        if ($ch eq '+' || $ch eq '-') {
            $i++;
            my $n = "";
            while ($i <= $#c && $c[$i] =~ /\d/) { $n .= $c[$i]; $i++; }
            $i += int($n);
            next;
        }
        if ($ch eq '*' || $ch eq '#') { $i++; next; }
        if ($ch eq '.' || $ch eq ',') { $count{$ref}++; $i++; next; }
        $count{uc($ch)}++;
        $i++;
    }

    my $total = 0;
    foreach my $b (qw(A C G T N)) { $total += $count{$b} if $count{$b}; }
    return $ref if $total == 0;

    foreach my $b (qw(A C G T)) {
        next if $b eq $ref;
        return $b if defined $count{$b} && $count{$b} / $total > $threshold;
    }
    return $ref;
}

sub shellQuote {
    my ($s) = @_;
    $s =~ s/'/'\\''/g;
    return "'$s'";
}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 07:58:27Z)**_

Done. New behavior in [bin/breakpointPileup.pl](bin/breakpointPileup.pl):

- Without `--guideFile`: same as before — one FASTA record per breakpoint region, on the reference + strand.
- With `--guideFile guides.txt` (the same `group<TAB>chr:pos<TAB>+/-` input that `guidesToBreakpoints.pl` reads):
  - All breakpoint regions are pileup'd once.
  - Each guide entry is matched to a region by chromosome (and the guide pos must fall inside the region; if no range hits, falls back to first region on the same chr with a warning).
  - The matched consensus is reverse-complemented when the guide direction is `-`.
  - All parts in the group are concatenated **in the order they appear in the guides file** (first listed = 5' partner) and emitted as a single FASTA record named after the group.

Run:

```
bin/filterEnds.pl --first first.bam --last last.bam -b breakpoints.txt > reads.txt
bin/breakpointPileup.pl --reads reads.txt --bam all.bam --ref ref.fa \
                       -b breakpoints.txt -G guides.txt > fusion.fa
```

A couple of things to watch:

- The "5' partner first" rule comes from line order in the guides file — if your guides file lists the 3' partner first for a given group, the FASTA will come out reversed. Easy to flip with row order; let me know if you'd rather drive it from a column instead.
- Match-by-position-within-range relies on the breakpoint file's range actually bracketing the guide pos. If your guide positions sit outside the listed `chr:start:end`, the script falls back to the first region on that chromosome and warns — worth eyeballing the warnings on the first run.
- Reverse complement is plain base-flip + reverse on `ACGTN`; any other characters (shouldn't happen from mpileup output) pass through unchanged.
